title: Production-Ready Python AI Project Template
desc: Bootstrap a production-ready AI project with .env management, logging, retry wrappers, and Dockerfile from scratch.

sections.body: A well-structured AI project has distinct layers: config, clients, tools, and tests.

code.label: bash - create project skeleton
code.src:
mkdir ai_project && cd ai_project
mkdir -p src/{clients,tools,prompts} tests
touch src/__init__.py src/config.py src/clients/openai_client.py
touch tests/test_clients.py .env .gitignore Dockerfile requirements.txt
echo '.env
*.pyc
__pycache__/
.venv/' > .gitignore

sections.body: All env vars loaded and validated at startup.

code.label: Python - config.py

sections.body: A reusable client with exponential backoff.

code.label: Python - openai_client.py
code.src:
import time, random
from openai import OpenAI, RateLimitError
from src.config import config

client = OpenAI(api_key=config.openai_key)

def call(messages: list, **kwargs) -> str:
    for attempt in range(5):
        try:
            resp = client.chat.completions.create(
                model=config.model,
                messages=messages,
                max_tokens=config.max_tokens,
                **kwargs
            )
            return resp.choices[0].message.content
        except RateLimitError:
            wait = 2**attempt + random.random()
            time.sleep(min(wait, 60))
    raise Exception('Max retries exceeded')

title: Multi-Turn Chatbot with Memory
desc: Build a stateful chatbot that maintains conversation history, trims context automatically, and streams responses to a browser via WebSockets.

sections.body: Maintain history and auto-trim to avoid context overflow.

code.label: Python - conversation.py

sections.body: Stream tokens to the browser in real time.

code.label: Python - app.py
code.src:
from fastapi import FastAPI, WebSocket
from openai import AsyncOpenAI
from src.conversation import Conversation

app = FastAPI()
async_client = AsyncOpenAI()

@app.websocket('/ws')
async def ws_chat(ws: WebSocket):
    await ws.accept()
    conv = Conversation(system='You are a helpful AI assistant.')
    while True:
        user_msg = await ws.receive_text()
        conv.add('user', user_msg)
        buffer = ''
        async with async_client.chat.completions.stream(
            model='gpt-4o-mini', messages=conv.messages()
        ) as stream:
            async for event in stream:
                token = event.choices[0].delta.content or ''
                if token:
                    buffer += token
                    await ws.send_text(token)
        conv.add('assistant', buffer)

title: Email Draft Assistant
desc: Streamlit app that rewrites emails in different tones (professional, casual, concise) with one-click copy.

sections.body: Rewrite any email in the requested tone.

code.label: Python - app.py
code.src:
import streamlit as st
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI()
TONES = ['professional','concise','friendly','firm','apologetic']

st.title('✉️ Email Tone Rewriter')
original = st.text_area('Paste your email', height=200)
tone = st.selectbox('Target tone', TONES)

if st.button('Rewrite') and original:
    with st.spinner('Rewriting...'):
        resp = client.chat.completions.create(
            model='gpt-4o-mini',
            messages=[{
                'role':'system',
                'content':f'Rewrite the email in a {tone} tone. Preserve the core message. Output only the rewritten email.'
            },{'role':'user','content':original}]
        )
        result = resp.choices[0].message.content
    st.text_area('Rewritten', value=result, height=200)
    st.code(result, language=None)

title: AI-Powered Price Monitoring Agent
desc: Web agent that monitors competitor prices, detects changes, and generates natural-language price intelligence reports.

sections.body: Scrape prices and use GPT-4o to write competitive intelligence.

code.label: Python - price_monitor.py
code.src:
from playwright.async_api import async_playwright
from openai import OpenAI
import sqlite3, json, asyncio

client = OpenAI()

async def scrape_price(url: str) -> str:
    async with async_playwright() as p:
        browser = await p.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        page = await browser.new_page()
        await page.goto(url, timeout=15000)
        content = await page.inner_text('body')
        await browser.close()
    return content[:5000]

def analyze_prices(scraped: dict) -> str:
    context = json.dumps(scraped, indent=2)
    return client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o',
        messages=[{
            'role':'system',
            'content':'Analyze these product prices and write a 3-bullet competitive intelligence summary with price comparisons and recommendations.'
        },{'role':'user','content':context}]
    ).choices[0].message.content

title: AI Resume Screening System
desc: Parse resumes, extract structured candidate data, score fit against a job description, and rank applicants.

sections.body: Extract and score candidate profiles against a job description.

code.label: Python - resume.py
code.src:
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import Optional
from openai import OpenAI
import json

client = OpenAI()

class Candidate(BaseModel):
    name: str
    email: Optional[str] = None
    years_experience: float
    skills: list[str]
    education: str
    fit_score: float  # 0-10
    fit_reasons: list[str]
    concerns: list[str]

def screen_resume(resume_text: str, job_description: str) -> Candidate:
    resp = client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o',
        response_format={'type':'json_object'},
        messages=[{
            'role':'system',
            'content':'Extract candidate info and score their fit for the job. JSON: {name,email,years_experience,skills,education,fit_score,fit_reasons,concerns}'
        },{'role':'user','content':f'JOB:\n{job_description}\n\nRESUME:\n{resume_text}'}]
    )
    return Candidate(**json.loads(resp.choices[0].message.content))

title: LLM Prompt A/B Testing Framework
desc: Systematically compare two prompts across 100+ test cases and identify which performs better with statistical significance.

sections.body: Run both prompts on the same inputs and score with an LLM judge.

code.label: Python - ab_test.py
code.src:
from openai import OpenAI
import json, asyncio

client = OpenAI()

async def eval_pair(input_text: str, response_a: str, response_b: str, criteria: str) -> dict:
    """LLM-as-judge: which response is better?"""
    resp = client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o',
        response_format={'type':'json_object'},
        messages=[{
            'role':'system',
            'content':f'Evaluate two AI responses by: {criteria}. JSON: {{winner:"A"|"B"|"tie",score_a:1-10,score_b:1-10,reasoning:str}}'
        },{'role':'user','content':f'INPUT: {input_text}\n\nRESPONSE A:\n{response_a}\n\nRESPONSE B:\n{response_b}'}]
    )
    return json.loads(resp.choices[0].message.content)

title: Autonomous Research Agent
desc: An agent that researches any topic by searching the web, reading pages, taking notes in a vector store, and writing a structured report.

sections.body: Agent accumulates notes in ChromaDB across multiple search rounds.

code.label: Python - research_agent.py
code.src:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_community.tools.tavily_search import TavilySearchResults
from langchain_community.vectorstores import Chroma
from langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings
from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor, create_openai_tools_agent
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate

llm   = ChatOpenAI(model='gpt-4o', temperature=0)
tools = [TavilySearchResults(max_results=5)]

prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
    ('system', 'You are a research agent. Search for information, synthesize findings, and write comprehensive reports. Always cite your sources.'),
    ('user', '{input}'),
    ('placeholder', '{agent_scratchpad}')
])

agent = create_openai_tools_agent(llm, tools, prompt)
executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, tools=tools, verbose=True, max_iterations=10)

result = executor.invoke({'input': 'Research the latest developments in reasoning LLMs (2025-2026) and write a 500-word report.'})
print(result['output'])

title: Coding Agent with Code Execution
desc: Agent that writes Python code, executes it in a sandboxed Docker container, reads the output, and iterates until the task is complete.

sections.body: Execute LLM-generated code safely in Docker.

code.label: Python - coding_agent.py
code.src:
import subprocess, tempfile, os
from openai import OpenAI
import json

client = OpenAI()

def run_code_in_sandbox(code: str, timeout: int = 30) -> dict:
    """Execute Python code in an isolated Docker container."""
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.py', mode='w', delete=False) as f:
        f.write(code)
        code_file = f.name
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ['docker','run','--rm','--network=none',
             '--memory=256m','--cpus=0.5',
             '-v',f'{code_file}:/code.py',
             'python:3.12-slim','python','/code.py'],
            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=timeout
        )
        return {'stdout':result.stdout,'stderr':result.stderr,'exit_code':result.returncode}
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
        return {'stdout':'','stderr':'Timeout exceeded','exit_code':1}
    finally:
        os.unlink(code_file)

title: Multi-Agent Customer Support System
desc: A multi-agent system with a router, knowledge-base agent, billing agent, and escalation agent. Routes tickets intelligently.

sections.body: Route tickets to specialised agents using LangGraph conditional edges.

code.label: Python - support_graph.py
code.src:
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, END
from typing import TypedDict, Literal
from openai import OpenAI
import json

client = OpenAI()

class TicketState(TypedDict):
    ticket: str
    category: str
    response: str
    escalated: bool

def classify(state: TicketState) -> TicketState:
    resp = client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o-mini',
        messages=[{'role':'user','content':f'Classify ticket as billing|technical|general: {state["ticket"]}'}],
        response_format={'type':'json_object'}
    )
    cat = json.loads(resp.choices[0].message.content).get('category','general')
    return {**state, 'category': cat}

def route(state: TicketState) -> Literal['billing','technical','general']:
    return state['category']

graph = StateGraph(TicketState)
graph.add_node('classify', classify)
graph.set_entry_point('classify')
graph.add_conditional_edges('classify', route)

title: Production RAG Pipeline with Evaluation
desc: Full production RAG system with hybrid search, re-ranking, citation, and automated quality evaluation using RAGAS.

sections.body: Combine vector and keyword search, then re-rank for precision.

code.label: Python - rag_pipeline.py
code.src:
from langchain.retrievers import BM25Retriever, EnsembleRetriever
from langchain_community.vectorstores import FAISS
from langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings
from sentence_transformers import CrossEncoder

embeds = OpenAIEmbeddings(model='text-embedding-3-small')
reranker = CrossEncoder('cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2')

def build_retriever(docs):
    vs   = FAISS.from_documents(docs, embeds)
    bm25 = BM25Retriever.from_documents(docs)
    bm25.k = 10
    return EnsembleRetriever(
        retrievers=[bm25, vs.as_retriever(search_kwargs={'k':10})],
        weights=[0.3, 0.7]
    )

def retrieve_and_rerank(query: str, retriever, k: int = 5) -> list:
    candidates = retriever.invoke(query)
    scores = reranker.predict([(query, d.page_content) for d in candidates])
    ranked = sorted(zip(scores, candidates), key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
    return [doc for _, doc in ranked[:k]]

title: Domain-Specific Fine-Tuned Model
desc: Fine-tune Llama 3.2 on domain-specific Q&A data using LoRA/QLoRA, evaluate against the base model, and deploy via Ollama.

sections.body: Full fine-tuning run from dataset to exported GGUF model.

code.label: Python - finetune.py
code.src:
from unsloth import FastLanguageModel
from datasets import Dataset
from trl import SFTTrainer
from transformers import TrainingArguments

model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(
    model_name='unsloth/llama-3.2-8b-instruct',
    max_seq_length=2048, load_in_4bit=True
)
model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(
    model, r=16, lora_alpha=16,
    target_modules=['q_proj','k_proj','v_proj','o_proj'],
    use_gradient_checkpointing='unsloth'
)

def format_row(row):
    return {'text': f'<|user|>\n{row["question"]}\n<|assistant|>\n{row["answer"]}'}

data = Dataset.from_json('train.jsonl').map(format_row)

trainer = SFTTrainer(
    model=model, tokenizer=tokenizer, train_dataset=data,
    dataset_text_field='text', max_seq_length=2048,
    args=TrainingArguments(
        output_dir='./lora_out', num_train_epochs=3,
        per_device_train_batch_size=2, learning_rate=2e-4,
        fp16=True, logging_steps=10
    )
)
trainer.train()
model.save_pretrained_gguf('./model_gguf', tokenizer)

import asyncio, json, websockets, base64
from openai import AsyncOpenAI

async def voice_agent_session():
    client = AsyncOpenAI()
    # Connect to Realtime API
    async with client.beta.realtime.connect(
        model='gpt-4o-realtime-preview'
    ) as conn:
        # Configure session
        await conn.session.update(session={
            'modalities': ['audio','text'],
            'instructions': 'You are a helpful voice assistant.',
            'voice': 'alloy',
            'turn_detection': {'type':'server_vad'},
            'tools': [{
                'type':'function',
                'name':'get_weather',
                'description':'Get current weather',
                'parameters':{'type':'object','properties':{'city':{'type':'string'}},'required':['city']}
            }]
        })
        print('Voice session active — speak now')
        async for event in conn:
            if event.type == 'response.audio.delta':
                audio = base64.b64decode(event.delta)
                # send to audio output device

asyncio.run(voice_agent_session())

title: Multimodal Document Understanding System
desc: Process documents with mixed content (text, charts, tables, images) using GPT-4o vision. Extracts structured data from visual elements.

sections.body: Render each PDF page as an image and extract all content types.

code.label: Python - multimodal_doc.py
code.src:
import pypdf, fitz  # PyMuPDF
from PIL import Image
from openai import OpenAI
import base64, io

client = OpenAI()

def pdf_page_to_base64(pdf_path: str, page_num: int) -> str:
    doc = fitz.open(pdf_path)
    page = doc.load_page(page_num)
    img = page.get_pixmap(dpi=150)
    img_bytes = img.tobytes('png')
    return base64.b64encode(img_bytes).decode()

def analyze_page(img_b64: str) -> dict:
    resp = client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o',
        messages=[{'role':'user','content':[
            {'type':'text','text':'Extract all content from this page. JSON: {text:str, tables:[{headers,rows}], charts:[{type,title,data_summary}], key_figures:[str]}'},
            {'type':'image_url','image_url':{'url':f'data:image/png;base64,{img_b64}','detail':'high'}}
        ]}],
        response_format={'type':'json_object'},
        max_tokens=2000
    )
    return resp.choices[0].message.content

title: AI Workflow Automation Engine
desc: Drag-and-drop style workflow builder where each node is an AI operation. Execute complex multi-step pipelines with branching logic.

sections.body: Define and execute complex workflows as LangGraph state machines.

code.label: Python - workflow_engine.py
code.src:
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, END
from typing import TypedDict, Any
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI()

class WorkflowState(TypedDict):
    input: str
    extracted_data: dict
    transformed: str
    output: str
    errors: list[str]

def extract_node(state: WorkflowState) -> WorkflowState:
    resp = client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o',
        response_format={'type':'json_object'},
        messages=[{'role':'user','content':f'Extract key facts as JSON from: {state["input"]}'}]
    )
    return {**state, 'extracted_data': resp.choices[0].message.content}

def transform_node(state: WorkflowState) -> WorkflowState:
    return {**state, 'transformed': state['extracted_data']}

graph = StateGraph(WorkflowState)
graph.add_node('extract', extract_node)
graph.add_node('transform', transform_node)
graph.set_entry_point('extract')
graph.add_edge('extract', 'transform')
graph.add_edge('transform', END)

title: AI Personalization Engine
desc: Learn user preferences from interactions and personalise AI responses. Maintains user preference profiles updated in real time.

sections.body: Extract and update user preferences from every interaction.

code.label: Python - personalization.py
code.src:
from openai import OpenAI
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import Optional
import json, redis

client = OpenAI()
cache  = redis.Redis(host='localhost', decode_responses=True)

class UserProfile(BaseModel):
    user_id: str
    interests: list[str] = []
    preferred_depth: str = 'intermediate'  # beginner|intermediate|expert
    preferred_format: str = 'prose'        # prose|bullets|code
    language: str = 'English'
    context_notes: list[str] = []

def get_profile(user_id: str) -> UserProfile:
    data = cache.get(f'profile:{user_id}')
    if data:
        return UserProfile(**json.loads(data))
    return UserProfile(user_id=user_id)

def update_profile(profile: UserProfile, message: str) -> UserProfile:
    resp = client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o-mini',
        response_format={'type':'json_object'},
        messages=[{
            'role':'system',
            'content':'Extract any user preferences from this message. JSON: {interests:[],preferred_depth:str,preferred_format:str,notes:str}'
        },{'role':'user','content':message}]
    )
    updates = json.loads(resp.choices[0].message.content)
    profile.interests.extend(updates.get('interests',[]))
    cache.setex(f'profile:{profile.user_id}', 86400, profile.json())
    return profile

title: Synthetic Training Data Generator
desc: Generate high-quality synthetic training datasets for fine-tuning LLMs. Includes diversity controls and quality filtering.

sections.body: Generate Q&A pairs with controlled diversity and quality filtering.

code.label: Python - synthetic_data.py
code.src:
from openai import OpenAI
from pydantic import BaseModel
import json, asyncio

client = OpenAI()

class QAPair(BaseModel):
    question: str
    answer: str
    difficulty: str
    topic: str
    quality_score: float  # 0-1 from LLM judge

async def generate_batch(
    topic: str,
    difficulty: str,
    count: int = 10
) -> list[QAPair]:
    resp = client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o',
        response_format={'type':'json_object'},
        messages=[{
            'role':'system',
            'content':f'Generate {count} diverse Q&A pairs about {topic} at {difficulty} level. Each must be unique and specific. JSON: {{pairs: [{{question,answer,difficulty,topic}}]}}'
        },{'role':'user','content':f'Topic: {topic}, Difficulty: {difficulty}'}]
    )
    pairs_data = json.loads(resp.choices[0].message.content).get('pairs',[])
    return [QAPair(**p, quality_score=0.9) for p in pairs_data]

title: LLM Observability Dashboard
desc: Production observability for LLM apps: trace every call, measure latency, cost, and quality, and alert on anomalies.

sections.body: Instrument every LLM call with tracing and metrics.

code.label: Python - observability.py
code.src:
from langsmith import traceable
from openai import OpenAI
from prometheus_client import Counter, Histogram, start_http_server
import time

LLM_CALLS     = Counter('llm_calls_total', 'Total LLM API calls', ['model','status'])
LLM_LATENCY   = Histogram('llm_latency_seconds', 'LLM call latency', ['model'])
LLM_TOKENS    = Counter('llm_tokens_total', 'Tokens used', ['model','type'])
LLM_COST_USD  = Counter('llm_cost_usd_total', 'Estimated cost in USD', ['model'])

client = OpenAI()

@traceable(name='chat_completion')
def tracked_llm_call(messages: list, model='gpt-4o-mini') -> str:
    start = time.time()
    try:
        resp = client.chat.completions.create(model=model, messages=messages)
        latency = time.time() - start
        LLM_CALLS.labels(model=model, status='success').inc()
        LLM_LATENCY.labels(model=model).observe(latency)
        LLM_TOKENS.labels(model=model,type='prompt').inc(resp.usage.prompt_tokens)
        LLM_TOKENS.labels(model=model,type='completion').inc(resp.usage.completion_tokens)
        return resp.choices[0].message.content
    except Exception as e:
        LLM_CALLS.labels(model=model, status='error').inc()
        raise

code.label: Python - debate.py

title: LLM Task Decomposition Planner
desc: Given a complex goal, the agent breaks it into a dependency graph of subtasks, executes them in topological order, and aggregates results.

sections.body: Decompose a goal into subtasks with dependencies.

code.label: Python - task_planner.py
code.src:
from openai import OpenAI
from pydantic import BaseModel
import json, networkx as nx

client = OpenAI()

class Task(BaseModel):
    id: str
    name: str
    description: str
    depends_on: list[str] = []
    status: str = 'pending'
    result: str = ''

def decompose(goal: str) -> list[Task]:
    resp = client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o',
        response_format={'type':'json_object'},
        messages=[{
            'role':'system',
            'content':'Decompose this goal into 5-8 subtasks with dependencies. JSON: {tasks:[{id,name,description,depends_on:[]}]}'
        },{'role':'user','content':goal}]
    )
    return [Task(**t) for t in json.loads(resp.choices[0].message.content)['tasks']]

def build_dag(tasks: list[Task]) -> nx.DiGraph:
    G = nx.DiGraph()
    for t in tasks:
        G.add_node(t.id, task=t)
        for dep in t.depends_on:
            G.add_edge(dep, t.id)
    return G

sections.body: Detect and block unsafe inputs before they reach the LLM.

code.label: Python - guardrails.py

title: Autonomous Web Scraper Agent
desc: Agent that navigates websites autonomously, extracts structured data, handles pagination, and adapts to layout changes.

sections.body: Use GPT-4o vision to identify UI elements and navigate.

code.label: Python - scraper_agent.py
code.src:
from playwright.async_api import async_playwright, Page
from openai import OpenAI
import base64, json

client = OpenAI()

async def screenshot_b64(page: Page) -> str:
    buf = await page.screenshot(full_page=False)
    return base64.b64encode(buf).decode()

async def decide_next_action(page: Page, goal: str) -> dict:
    img = await screenshot_b64(page)
    resp = client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o',
        messages=[{'role':'user','content':[
            {'type':'text','text':f'Goal: {goal}. What to click/type next? JSON: {{action:click|type|scroll|done, selector:str, value:str}}'},
            {'type':'image_url','image_url':{'url':f'data:image/png;base64,{img}'}}
        ]}],
        response_format={'type':'json_object'}
    )
    return json.loads(resp.choices[0].message.content)

async def scrape_agent(url: str, goal: str) -> list[dict]:
    async with async_playwright() as p:
        browser = await p.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        page = await browser.new_page()
        await page.goto(url)
        for _ in range(20):  # max 20 actions
            action = await decide_next_action(page, goal)
            if action['action'] == 'done': break
            elif action['action'] == 'click': await page.click(action['selector'])
            elif action['action'] == 'type':  await page.fill(action['selector'], action['value'])
        content = await page.content()
        await browser.close()
    return content

title: AI Anomaly Detection System
desc: Combine statistical anomaly detection with LLM-generated natural language explanations for every detected anomaly.

code.label: Python - anomaly.py

title: Scikit-learn ML Pipeline with Cross-Validation
desc: Build a production-ready sklearn pipeline with feature engineering, preprocessing, cross-validation, hyperparameter tuning with Optuna, and SHAP explainability.

sections.body: Sklearn pipelines chain preprocessing and modelling into one serializable object — preventing data leakage and simplifying deployment.

code.label: Python - sklearn Pipeline with ColumnTransformer + Optuna
code.src:
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, OneHotEncoder
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.ensemble import GradientBoostingClassifier
from sklearn.model_selection import cross_val_score, StratifiedKFold
import optuna, joblib

numeric_features     = ['age', 'income', 'tenure_days']
categorical_features = ['plan_type', 'region', 'device']

numeric_transformer = Pipeline([
    ('impute', SimpleImputer(strategy='median')),
    ('scale',  StandardScaler()),
])
categorical_transformer = Pipeline([
    ('impute', SimpleImputer(strategy='most_frequent')),
    ('ohe',    OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown='ignore', sparse_output=False)),
])
preprocessor = ColumnTransformer([
    ('num', numeric_transformer,      numeric_features),
    ('cat', categorical_transformer,  categorical_features),
])

clf = Pipeline([
    ('preprocessor', preprocessor),
    ('classifier', GradientBoostingClassifier(random_state=42))
])

def objective(trial):
    clf.set_params(
        classifier__n_estimators  = trial.suggest_int('n_est', 50, 500),
        classifier__max_depth     = trial.suggest_int('depth', 2, 8),
        classifier__learning_rate = trial.suggest_float('lr', 0.01, 0.3, log=True),
        classifier__subsample     = trial.suggest_float('sub', 0.5, 1.0),
    )
    cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)
    return cross_val_score(clf, X_train, y_train, cv=cv, scoring='roc_auc', n_jobs=-1).mean()

study = optuna.create_study(direction='maximize')
study.optimize(objective, n_trials=50, show_progress_bar=True)
print(f'Best AUC: {study.best_value:.4f}')
clf.set_params(**{f'classifier__{k}': v for k, v in study.best_params.items()})
clf.fit(X_train, y_train)
joblib.dump(clf, 'churn_pipeline.joblib')

sections.body: SHAP gives per-prediction feature importance — essential for model explainability in production.

code.label: Python - SHAP explainability on GBM pipeline
code.src:
import shap, numpy as np

explainer = shap.TreeExplainer(clf.named_steps['classifier'])
X_test_proc = clf.named_steps['preprocessor'].transform(X_test)

cat_names = list(
    clf.named_steps['preprocessor']
       .named_transformers_['cat']
       .named_steps['ohe']
       .get_feature_names_out(categorical_features)
)
all_features = numeric_features + list(cat_names)

shap_values = explainer.shap_values(X_test_proc)
print('Top 5 features:')
mean_abs = np.abs(shap_values).mean(axis=0)
for idx in np.argsort(mean_abs)[::-1][:5]:
    print(f'  {all_features[idx]:30s}  mean|SHAP| = {mean_abs[idx]:.4f}')

title: Production Text Classifier: TF-IDF to DistilBERT
desc: Build a text classification system starting with a TF-IDF + Logistic Regression baseline, then upgrade to fine-tuned DistilBERT. Compare accuracy vs latency trade-offs.

sections.body: Always build a fast baseline first. TF-IDF + Logistic Regression achieves surprisingly high accuracy and trains in seconds.

code.label: Python - TF-IDF + LR baseline classifier
code.src:
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
import time

X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
    texts, labels, test_size=0.2, random_state=42, stratify=labels
)

baseline = Pipeline([
    ('tfidf', TfidfVectorizer(
        max_features=50000, ngram_range=(1,2),
        sublinear_tf=True, strip_accents='unicode', min_df=2
    )),
    ('clf', LogisticRegression(C=5.0, max_iter=1000, n_jobs=-1))
])

t0 = time.time()
baseline.fit(X_train, y_train)
train_time = time.time() - t0

t0 = time.time()
preds = baseline.predict(X_test)
infer_ms = (time.time() - t0) / len(X_test) * 1000

print(classification_report(y_test, preds))
print(f'Train: {train_time:.1f}s | Infer: {infer_ms:.3f}ms/sample')

sections.body: Fine-tune DistilBERT using HuggingFace Trainer. This typically adds 5-15 accuracy points over TF-IDF on domain text.

code.label: Python - DistilBERT fine-tuning with HuggingFace Trainer

title: Hybrid Recommendation Engine
desc: Combine collaborative filtering (matrix factorization SVD) with content-based sentence embeddings for a hybrid recommender that handles cold start.

sections.body: Matrix factorization decomposes the user-item matrix into latent factor representations, capturing implicit preferences.

code.label: Python - SVD collaborative filtering with scikit-surprise
code.src:
from surprise import SVD, Dataset, Reader
from surprise.model_selection import cross_validate, GridSearchCV
import pandas as pd

ratings_df = pd.read_csv('ratings.csv')  # user_id, item_id, rating
reader = Reader(rating_scale=(1, 5))
data = Dataset.load_from_df(ratings_df[['user_id','item_id','rating']], reader)

param_grid = {
    'n_factors': [50, 100, 200],
    'n_epochs':  [20, 40],
    'lr_all':    [0.002, 0.005],
    'reg_all':   [0.02, 0.1]
}
gs = GridSearchCV(SVD, param_grid, measures=['rmse'], cv=5, n_jobs=-1)
gs.fit(data)
print(f'Best RMSE: {gs.best_score["rmse"]:.4f}')

best_svd = SVD(**gs.best_params['rmse'])
best_svd.fit(data.build_full_trainset())

def predict_rating(user_id, item_id) -> float:
    return round(best_svd.predict(user_id, item_id).est, 2)

sections.body: New items with no ratings get zero collaborative signal. Content embeddings handle cold start by comparing item descriptions semantically.

code.label: Python - sentence-transformer content similarity
code.src:
from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
import numpy as np
from sklearn.metrics.pairwise import cosine_similarity
import pandas as pd

items = pd.read_csv('items.csv')  # id, title, description
model = SentenceTransformer('all-MiniLM-L6-v2')

embeddings = model.encode(
    items['description'].tolist(), batch_size=64,
    show_progress_bar=True, normalize_embeddings=True
)
np.save('item_embeddings.npy', embeddings)

def content_similar(item_id: str, k: int = 10) -> list:
    idx  = items.index[items['id'] == item_id][0]
    sims = cosine_similarity(embeddings[idx:idx+1], embeddings)[0]
    top  = np.argsort(sims)[::-1][1:k+1]
    return [{'id': items.iloc[i]['id'], 'score': float(sims[i])} for i in top]

print(content_similar('item_42', k=5))

title: Time Series Forecasting: SARIMA to NeuralProphet
desc: Forecast product demand with SARIMA statistical baseline and NeuralProphet neural model. Add external regressors (holidays, weekends) and compare MAPE.

sections.body: Always start with the statistical baseline. SARIMA handles trend and weekly seasonality with no GPU needed.

code.label: Python - SARIMA with weekly seasonality
code.src:
import pandas as pd
from statsmodels.tsa.statespace.sarimax import SARIMAX
from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_percentage_error
import warnings; warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

df = pd.read_csv('sales.csv', parse_dates=['date'], index_col='date')
df = df.asfreq('D').fillna(method='ffill')

train, test = df.iloc[:-30], df.iloc[-30:]

model = SARIMAX(train['sales'], order=(1,1,1),
                seasonal_order=(1,1,1,7),
                enforce_stationarity=False).fit(disp=False)
forecast = model.forecast(steps=30)
print(f'SARIMA MAPE: {mean_absolute_percentage_error(test["sales"], forecast):.2%}')

sections.body: NeuralProphet extends Prophet with neural networks and lag features. It outperforms SARIMA on complex seasonal patterns.

code.label: Python - NeuralProphet with holiday regressors
code.src:
from neuralprophet import NeuralProphet
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_percentage_error

np_df = df.reset_index().rename(columns={'date':'ds','sales':'y'})
np_df['is_weekend'] = (np_df['ds'].dt.dayofweek >= 5).astype(int)
np_df['is_holiday'] = np_df['ds'].isin(holiday_dates).astype(int)

train_np, test_np = np_df.iloc[:-30], np_df.iloc[-30:]

model = NeuralProphet(
    n_forecasts=30, n_lags=14,
    seasonality_mode='multiplicative',
    weekly_seasonality=True, yearly_seasonality=True,
    learning_rate=0.01, epochs=100, batch_size=32,
)
model.add_lagged_regressor('is_weekend')
model.add_lagged_regressor('is_holiday')
model.fit(train_np, freq='D')

forecast = model.predict(model.make_future_dataframe(train_np, periods=30))
mape = mean_absolute_percentage_error(test_np['y'], forecast.tail(30)['yhat1'])
print(f'NeuralProphet MAPE: {mape:.2%}')

title: Anomaly Detection: Isolation Forest + Autoencoder
desc: Detect anomalies in transaction data using Isolation Forest (statistical) and a PyTorch Autoencoder (neural). Compare ROC-AUC and serve via FastAPI.

sections.body: Isolation Forest isolates anomalies by random partitioning. Anomalies need fewer splits to isolate — they get lower anomaly scores.

code.label: Python - Isolation Forest unsupervised anomaly detection
code.src:
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.ensemble import IsolationForest
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score

features = ['amount','hour_of_day','days_since_last_txn',
            'merchant_cat_encoded','velocity_24h','distance_km']

X_train = pd.read_csv('txn_train.csv')[features]
df_test  = pd.read_csv('txn_test.csv')
X_test, y_test = df_test[features], df_test['is_fraud']

pipeline = Pipeline([
    ('scaler',    StandardScaler()),
    ('isoforest', IsolationForest(n_estimators=200, contamination=0.02, n_jobs=-1))
])
pipeline.fit(X_train)
scores = -pipeline.decision_function(X_test)  # higher = more anomalous
print(f'Isolation Forest ROC-AUC: {roc_auc_score(y_test, scores):.4f}')

sections.body: Train an autoencoder on normal transactions. Anomalies have high reconstruction error because the model never saw them during training.

code.label: Python (PyTorch) - autoencoder reconstruction error
code.src:
import torch, torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, TensorDataset
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score
import numpy as np

class Autoencoder(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, d):
        super().__init__()
        self.enc = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(d,32),nn.ReLU(),nn.Linear(32,8))
        self.dec = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(8,32),nn.ReLU(),nn.Linear(32,d))
    def forward(self, x): return self.dec(self.enc(x))

scaler = StandardScaler()
X_s = torch.FloatTensor(scaler.fit_transform(X_train.values))
loader = DataLoader(TensorDataset(X_s, X_s), batch_size=256, shuffle=True)

model = Autoencoder(X_train.shape[1])
opt   = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3)
for epoch in range(50):
    for xb, _ in loader:
        loss = nn.MSELoss()(model(xb), xb)
        opt.zero_grad(); loss.backward(); opt.step()

model.eval()
with torch.no_grad():
    Xt = torch.FloatTensor(scaler.transform(X_test.values))
    err = ((Xt - model(Xt))**2).mean(dim=1).numpy()
print(f'Autoencoder ROC-AUC: {roc_auc_score(y_test, err):.4f}')

title: Feature Store with Feast and Redis
desc: Eliminate training/serving skew by building a feature store with Feast. Define feature views, materialize to Redis online store, and serve features in real-time at sub-10ms latency.

sections.body: A feature store ensures the same feature logic runs at training and serving time — eliminating the most common source of ML production bugs.

code.label: Python - Feast feature view definition
code.src:
from feast import FeatureStore, Entity, FeatureView, Field, FileSource
from feast.types import Float32, Int64
from datetime import timedelta

user = Entity(name='user_id')
user_source = FileSource(
    path='data/user_features.parquet',
    timestamp_field='event_timestamp'
)
user_features_view = FeatureView(
    name='user_features', entities=[user],
    ttl=timedelta(days=7),
    schema=[
        Field(name='total_purchases_30d', dtype=Float32),
        Field(name='avg_order_value',     dtype=Float32),
        Field(name='days_since_last_visit', dtype=Int64),
        Field(name='churn_risk_score',    dtype=Float32),
    ],
    source=user_source, online=True,
)
store = FeatureStore(repo_path='feature_repo/')
store.apply([user, user_features_view])
print('Feature store configured.')

sections.body: Materialization copies features from the offline warehouse to Redis for real-time serving. Run this on a cron schedule.

code.label: Python - materialize + online feature serving
code.src:
from feast import FeatureStore
from datetime import datetime
import pandas as pd

store = FeatureStore(repo_path='feature_repo/')

# Materialize to Redis (run on schedule)
store.materialize_incremental(end_date=datetime.utcnow())
print('Materialized to Redis.')

# Historical features for training (point-in-time correct)
entity_df = pd.DataFrame({
    'user_id': ['u001', 'u002'],
    'event_timestamp': pd.to_datetime(['2024-01-15', '2024-01-16'])
})
train_features = store.get_historical_features(
    entity_df=entity_df,
    features=['user_features:total_purchases_30d',
              'user_features:churn_risk_score']
).to_df()
print(train_features)

# Online features for serving (< 10ms from Redis)
online = store.get_online_features(
    features=['user_features:total_purchases_30d','user_features:churn_risk_score'],
    entity_rows=[{'user_id': 'u001'}]
).to_dict()
print(f'Online features: {online}')

title: MLflow Experiment Tracking and Model Registry
desc: Track experiments with MLflow autolog, compare runs in the UI, register the best model, and serve it as a REST endpoint — the foundation of reproducible ML.

sections.body: MLflow autolog captures parameters, metrics, and model artifacts automatically. Manual logging adds custom metrics and plots.

code.label: Python - MLflow experiment tracking with autolog
code.src:
import mlflow, mlflow.sklearn
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

mlflow.set_tracking_uri('http://localhost:5000')
mlflow.set_experiment('churn-prediction-v2')

X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.2)

for n_est in [100, 200, 500]:
    with mlflow.start_run(run_name=f'rf_{n_est}_trees'):
        mlflow.log_params({'n_estimators': n_est, 'max_depth': 8,
                            'dataset_size': len(X_train)})
        clf = RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=n_est, max_depth=8,
                                      n_jobs=-1, random_state=42)
        clf.fit(X_train, y_train)
        auc = roc_auc_score(y_test, clf.predict_proba(X_test)[:,1])
        mlflow.log_metric('roc_auc', auc)
        from mlflow.models import infer_signature
        mlflow.sklearn.log_model(clf, 'model',
            signature=infer_signature(X_train, clf.predict(X_train)),
            registered_model_name='churn-rf')
        print(f'n_est={n_est}: AUC={auc:.4f}')

title: PyTorch Image Classifier with Mixed Precision Training
desc: Build a complete PyTorch training loop for image classification: custom Dataset, DataLoader with augmentation, AMP mixed precision, gradient clipping, and W&B logging.

sections.body: A proper Dataset handles augmentation, normalization, and edge cases. The DataLoader handles batching and parallel loading.

code.label: Python (PyTorch) - custom Dataset + transforms
code.src:
import torch
from torch.utils.data import Dataset, DataLoader
from torchvision import transforms
from pathlib import Path
from PIL import Image
import pandas as pd

class ImageDataset(Dataset):
    def __init__(self, df, img_dir, transform=None):
        self.df, self.img_dir = df.reset_index(drop=True), Path(img_dir)
        self.transform = transform
    def __len__(self): return len(self.df)
    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        row = self.df.iloc[idx]
        img = Image.open(self.img_dir / row['filename']).convert('RGB')
        return self.transform(img) if self.transform else img, int(row['label'])

train_tf = transforms.Compose([
    transforms.RandomResizedCrop(224, scale=(0.7,1.0)),
    transforms.RandomHorizontalFlip(),
    transforms.ColorJitter(0.3, 0.3, 0.2),
    transforms.ToTensor(),
    transforms.Normalize([0.485,0.456,0.406],[0.229,0.224,0.225]),
])
train_loader = DataLoader(ImageDataset(df_train,'images/',train_tf),
                           batch_size=64, shuffle=True, num_workers=4, pin_memory=True)

sections.body: Mixed precision (float16 forward, float32 weights) halves VRAM and doubles throughput on modern GPUs.

code.label: Python (PyTorch) - AMP training loop with OneCycleLR
code.src:
import torch, torch.nn as nn
from torch.cuda.amp import autocast, GradScaler
import timm, wandb

device = torch.device('cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu')
model  = timm.create_model('efficientnet_b0', pretrained=True,
                             num_classes=NUM_CLASSES).to(device)
model  = torch.compile(model)  # PyTorch 2.0 compile for +15% speed

optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01)
scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
    optimizer, max_lr=3e-4, steps_per_epoch=len(train_loader), epochs=20)
loss_fn = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)
scaler  = GradScaler()

wandb.init(project='image-clf')
for epoch in range(20):
    model.train()
    for imgs, labels in train_loader:
        imgs, labels = imgs.to(device), labels.to(device)
        optimizer.zero_grad()
        with autocast():
            loss = loss_fn(model(imgs), labels)
        scaler.scale(loss).backward()
        scaler.unscale_(optimizer)
        torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)
        scaler.step(optimizer); scaler.update(); scheduler.step()
    wandb.log({'epoch': epoch, 'lr': scheduler.get_last_lr()[0]})

title: Named Entity Recognition with BERT Token Classification
desc: Fine-tune BERT for NER with correct BIO label alignment across subword tokens. Evaluate with seqeval span-level F1, then export to ONNX for production.

sections.body: BERT subword tokenization breaks words into pieces. NER labels are per-word, so continuation subwords must be masked to avoid training noise.

code.label: Python - BIO label alignment for BERT NER

sections.body: seqeval computes span-level F1 — the standard NER metric. Per-entity breakdown reveals where the model needs more data.

code.label: Python - NER training + seqeval F1
code.src:
from transformers import (AutoModelForTokenClassification,
                          TrainingArguments, Trainer, DataCollatorForTokenClassification)
from evaluate import load
import numpy as np

model = AutoModelForTokenClassification.from_pretrained(
    'bert-base-cased', num_labels=len(label2id),
    id2label=id2label, label2id=label2id)

seqeval = load('seqeval')
def compute_metrics(p):
    preds  = np.argmax(p.predictions, axis=-1)
    true_p = [[id2label[x] for x,l in zip(pr,la) if l != -100]
               for pr,la in zip(preds, p.label_ids)]
    true_l = [[id2label[l] for l in la if l != -100] for la in p.label_ids]
    r = seqeval.compute(predictions=true_p, references=true_l)
    return {'f1': r['overall_f1'], 'precision': r['overall_precision'],
             'recall': r['overall_recall']}

Trainer(
    model=model,
    args=TrainingArguments('./bert-ner', num_train_epochs=5,
        per_device_train_batch_size=32, learning_rate=3e-5,
        evaluation_strategy='epoch', load_best_model_at_end=True, metric_for_best_model='f1'),
    train_dataset=train_ds, eval_dataset=val_ds,
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    data_collator=DataCollatorForTokenClassification(tokenizer),
    compute_metrics=compute_metrics,
).train()

title: GPT-Style Language Model from Scratch
desc: Build a complete autoregressive transformer LM from scratch: causal self-attention with mask, positional embeddings, weight tying, and a training loop on a small corpus.

sections.body: Autoregressive models mask future positions so each token can only attend to prior tokens. The causal mask is a lower-triangular matrix of ones.

code.label: Python (PyTorch) - GPT causal attention from scratch
code.src:
import torch, torch.nn as nn, torch.nn.functional as F

class CausalSelfAttention(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, d_model, n_heads, dropout=0.1, max_len=1024):
        super().__init__()
        self.n_heads = n_heads
        self.d_head  = d_model // n_heads
        self.scale   = self.d_head ** -0.5
        self.qkv  = nn.Linear(d_model, 3*d_model, bias=False)
        self.proj = nn.Linear(d_model, d_model, bias=False)
        self.drop = nn.Dropout(dropout)
        self.register_buffer('mask',
            torch.tril(torch.ones(max_len,max_len)).view(1,1,max_len,max_len))

    def forward(self, x):
        B, T, C = x.shape
        Q, K, V = self.qkv(x).split(C, dim=-1)
        def mh(t): return t.view(B,T,self.n_heads,self.d_head).transpose(1,2)
        Q, K, V = mh(Q), mh(K), mh(V)
        attn = (Q @ K.transpose(-2,-1)) * self.scale
        attn = attn.masked_fill(self.mask[:,:,:T,:T] == 0, float('-inf'))
        attn = self.drop(F.softmax(attn, dim=-1))
        out  = (attn @ V).transpose(1,2).contiguous().view(B,T,C)
        return self.proj(out)

sections.h2: Full GPT Model and Training Step
sections.body: Stack transformer blocks, add token + positional embeddings, tie the LM head weight to the embedding matrix, and train with cross-entropy on shifted sequences.

code.label: Python (PyTorch) - MiniGPT full model
code.src:
class GPTBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, d,h,ff,drop=0.1,ml=1024):
        super().__init__()
        self.ln1  = nn.LayerNorm(d)
        self.attn = CausalSelfAttention(d,h,drop,ml)
        self.ln2  = nn.LayerNorm(d)
        self.ff   = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(d,ff),nn.GELU(),nn.Dropout(drop),
                                   nn.Linear(ff,d),nn.Dropout(drop))
    def forward(self,x):
        x = x + self.attn(self.ln1(x))
        x = x + self.ff(self.ln2(x))
        return x

class MiniGPT(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, vocab_size, d=256, n=6, h=8, ff=1024, ml=512, drop=0.1):
        super().__init__()
        self.te = nn.Embedding(vocab_size, d)
        self.pe = nn.Embedding(ml, d)
        self.drop = nn.Dropout(drop)
        self.blocks = nn.ModuleList([GPTBlock(d,h,ff,drop,ml) for _ in range(n)])
        self.ln  = nn.LayerNorm(d)
        self.lmh = nn.Linear(d, vocab_size, bias=False)
        self.lmh.weight = self.te.weight  # weight tying

    def forward(self, idx, targets=None):
        B,T = idx.shape
        x = self.drop(self.te(idx) + self.pe(torch.arange(T,device=idx.device)))
        for block in self.blocks: x = block(x)
        logits = self.lmh(self.ln(x))
        loss = F.cross_entropy(logits.view(-1,logits.size(-1)),
                                targets.view(-1), ignore_index=-1) if targets is not None else None
        return logits, loss

model = MiniGPT(vocab_size=50257)
opt   = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=6e-4, betas=(0.9,0.95), weight_decay=0.1)
x, y  = next(iter(loader))
_, loss = model(x, y)
print(f'Initial loss: {loss.item():.4f}')  # ~ln(50257) ≈ 10.8

title: Knowledge Distillation: Teacher-Student Model Compression
desc: Compress a large BERT teacher into a small student using KL divergence distillation loss. Achieve 60% size reduction with < 5% accuracy loss, then export to ONNX for deployment.

sections.body: The student learns from teacher soft probabilities (KL divergence) and hard ground-truth labels. Temperature scaling softens the teacher distribution to transfer inter-class knowledge.

code.label: Python (PyTorch) - KL-divergence distillation loss
code.src:
import torch, torch.nn.functional as F

def distillation_loss(student_logits, teacher_logits, labels,
                       temperature=4.0, alpha=0.7):
    """
    L = alpha * KL(student_soft || teacher_soft) * T^2
      + (1-alpha) * CrossEntropy(student, hard_labels)
    temperature > 1 produces softer distributions revealing more structure.
    """
    s_soft = F.log_softmax(student_logits / temperature, dim=-1)
    t_soft = F.softmax(teacher_logits    / temperature, dim=-1)
    distill = F.kl_div(s_soft, t_soft, reduction='batchmean') * temperature**2
    hard    = F.cross_entropy(student_logits, labels)
    return alpha * distill + (1 - alpha) * hard

# Training step
teacher.eval()   # frozen
student.train()

for batch in dataloader:
    ids    = batch['input_ids'].to(device)
    labels = batch['labels'].to(device)
    with torch.no_grad():
        t_logits = teacher(ids).logits
    s_logits = student(ids).logits
    loss = distillation_loss(s_logits, t_logits, labels, temperature=4.0, alpha=0.7)
    loss.backward(); optimizer.step(); optimizer.zero_grad()

sections.body: Export the student to ONNX for production. ONNX Runtime with GPU typically provides 1.5-3x speedup over PyTorch eager mode.

code.label: Python - ONNX export + runtime benchmark
code.src:
import torch, onnx, onnxruntime as ort, time, numpy as np

student.eval()
dummy = (torch.randint(0,30522,(1,128)), torch.ones(1,128,dtype=torch.long))

torch.onnx.export(student, dummy, 'student.onnx',
    input_names=['input_ids','attention_mask'],
    output_names=['logits'],
    dynamic_axes={'input_ids':{0:'batch',1:'seq'},'attention_mask':{0:'batch',1:'seq'}},
    opset_version=17)
onnx.checker.check_model('student.onnx')
print('ONNX model valid.')

sess  = ort.InferenceSession('student.onnx',
    providers=['CUDAExecutionProvider','CPUExecutionProvider'])
batch = {'input_ids': dummy[0].numpy(), 'attention_mask': dummy[1].numpy()}

N = 200
t0 = time.perf_counter()
for _ in range(N): sess.run(None, batch)
orm_ms = (time.perf_counter()-t0)/N*1000

with torch.no_grad():
    t0 = time.perf_counter()
    for _ in range(N): student(*dummy)
    pt_ms = (time.perf_counter()-t0)/N*1000

print(f'PyTorch: {pt_ms:.2f}ms | ONNX: {orm_ms:.2f}ms | Speedup: {pt_ms/orm_ms:.2f}x')

title: High-Throughput LLM Inference Server with vLLM
desc: Deploy a production LLM server using vLLM's PagedAttention. Run up to 24x more concurrent requests than naive serving. Add OpenAI-compatible API and Prometheus metrics.

sections.body: PagedAttention manages KV cache like virtual memory — eliminating the need to pre-allocate maximum sequence length memory. This dramatically increases throughput and concurrency.

code.label: Python - vLLM offline batch inference with PagedAttention
code.src:
from vllm import LLM, SamplingParams
import time

llm = LLM(
    model='meta-llama/Llama-3.2-8B-Instruct',
    gpu_memory_utilization=0.90,
    max_num_seqs=256,      # concurrent sequences
    max_model_len=4096,
    quantization='awq',    # 4x memory reduction
)
params = SamplingParams(temperature=0.7, top_p=0.9, max_tokens=256)

prompts = [f'<|user|>\nExplain {t} in one paragraph.\n<|assistant|>\n'
            for t in ['attention', 'RAG', 'LoRA', 'RLHF', 'quantization',
                       'PagedAttention', 'DPO', 'chain-of-thought']]

t0 = time.time()
outs = llm.generate(prompts, params)
elapsed = time.time()-t0
tokens = sum(len(o.outputs[0].token_ids) for o in outs)
print(f'{len(prompts)} req | {tokens} tokens | {elapsed:.2f}s | {tokens/elapsed:.0f} tok/s')

sections.body: vLLM's built-in server exposes the OpenAI API spec — drop in any model behind the standard interface with no client changes.

code.label: bash + Python - vLLM OpenAI server + streaming client
code.src:
# Start server
python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \
  --model meta-llama/Llama-3.2-8B-Instruct \
  --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 \
  --gpu-memory-utilization 0.90 \
  --max-model-len 4096 \
  --api-key my-secret-key

# ── Python client (identical to OpenAI SDK) ──
from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI(base_url='http://localhost:8000/v1', api_key='my-secret-key')

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='meta-llama/Llama-3.2-8B-Instruct',
    messages=[{'role':'user','content':'What is PagedAttention?'}],
    temperature=0.7, max_tokens=256, stream=True
)
for chunk in response:
    if chunk.choices[0].delta.content:
        print(chunk.choices[0].delta.content, end='', flush=True)

title: LLM Evaluation Harness: MMLU, HumanEval & Custom Benchmarks
desc: Evaluate LLMs systematically with EleutherAI's harness for MMLU and HumanEval, then build a custom LLM-as-judge benchmark for domain-specific tasks with structured output scoring.

sections.body: EleutherAI's evaluation harness is the standard for open-source LLM benchmarking. Run MMLU (57 subjects), HumanEval (164 coding tasks), and hundreds of others with one command.

code.label: bash + Python - MMLU and HumanEval with lm-eval
code.src:
# Run MMLU (5-shot) and HumanEval (0-shot)
lm_eval --model hf \
  --model_args pretrained=meta-llama/Llama-3.2-8B-Instruct \
  --tasks mmlu,humaneval \
  --num_fewshot 5 \
  --batch_size 8 \
  --output_path results/llama3-benchmarks.json

# ── Parse results ──
import json, pandas as pd

with open('results/llama3-benchmarks.json') as f:
    data = json.load(f)

mmlu_subjects = {
    k.replace('mmlu_',''):v['acc,none']
    for k,v in data['results'].items()
    if k.startswith('mmlu_') and k != 'mmlu'
}
df = pd.DataFrame.from_dict(mmlu_subjects, orient='index', columns=['accuracy'])
print(df.sort_values('accuracy', ascending=False).head(10).to_string())
print(f'\nOverall MMLU:    {data["results"]["mmlu"]["acc,none"]:.1%}')
print(f'HumanEval pass@1: {data["results"]["humaneval"]["pass@1,none"]:.1%}')

sections.body: Standard benchmarks test general ability. A custom benchmark tests what your users actually need. Use GPT-4o as judge with structured output for reproducible scoring.

code.label: Python - custom LLM-as-judge with Pydantic structured output
code.src:
from openai import OpenAI
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import Literal
import json, pandas as pd

client = OpenAI()

class Score(BaseModel):
    score: int         # 1-5
    reasoning: str
    dimension: Literal['accuracy','completeness','clarity','safety']

JUDGE_SYS = ('Score the model response 1-5 on the given dimension. '
               'Return JSON only. 5=excellent, 1=incorrect/harmful.')

def evaluate(question, reference, response, dimension='accuracy') -> Score:
    result = client.beta.chat.completions.parse(
        model='gpt-4o', temperature=0.1,
        response_format=Score,
        messages=[
            {'role':'system','content': JUDGE_SYS},
            {'role':'user','content':
              f'Q: {question}\nREF: {reference}\nRESP: {response}\nDIM: {dimension}'}
        ]
    )
    return result.choices[0].message.parsed

test_cases = json.load(open('domain_eval.json'))
results = []
for tc in test_cases:
    resp = call_your_model(tc['question'])
    for dim in ['accuracy','completeness','clarity']:
        r = evaluate(tc['question'], tc['reference'], resp, dim)
        results.append({'id':tc['id'],'dim':dim,'score':r.score})

df = pd.DataFrame(results)
print(df.groupby('dim')['score'].mean().round(2))

title: Fine-tune Stable Diffusion with DreamBooth
desc: Fine-tune Stable Diffusion to generate custom subject images using DreamBooth. Learn about text conditioning, U-Net fine-tuning, and prior preservation loss to prevent forgetting.

sections.body: DreamBooth binds a rare token ('sks') to a subject using 5-10 images. Prior preservation loss prevents the model from forgetting the general class.

code.label: Python - DreamBooth training dataset + prior preservation
code.src:
from diffusers import StableDiffusionPipeline
from torch.utils.data import Dataset
from torchvision import transforms
from pathlib import Path
from PIL import Image
import torch

INSTANCE_PROMPT = 'a photo of sks dog'
CLASS_PROMPT    = 'a photo of a dog'

class DreamBoothDataset(Dataset):
    def __init__(self, instance_dir, class_dir, tokenizer, size=512):
        self.inst = sorted(Path(instance_dir).glob('*.jpg'))
        self.cls  = sorted(Path(class_dir).glob('*.jpg'))
        self.tok  = tokenizer
        self.tf   = transforms.Compose([
            transforms.Resize(size), transforms.CenterCrop(size),
            transforms.ToTensor(), transforms.Normalize([0.5],[0.5])
        ])
    def __len__(self): return len(self.inst)
    def __getitem__(self, i):
        inst_img = self.tf(Image.open(self.inst[i]).convert('RGB'))
        cls_img  = self.tf(Image.open(self.cls[i%len(self.cls)]).convert('RGB'))
        inst_ids = self.tok(INSTANCE_PROMPT,truncation=True,return_tensors='pt').input_ids[0]
        cls_ids  = self.tok(CLASS_PROMPT,   truncation=True,return_tensors='pt').input_ids[0]
        return {'instance_pixel':inst_img,'class_pixel':cls_img,
                'instance_ids':inst_ids,'class_ids':cls_ids}

sections.body: After training, use the instance token in any prompt to generate the subject in new contexts — paintings, scenes, styles.

code.label: Python - DreamBooth inference across styles
code.src:
from diffusers import StableDiffusionPipeline
import torch

pipe = StableDiffusionPipeline.from_pretrained(
    './dreambooth-output', torch_dtype=torch.float16, safety_checker=None
).to('cuda')

prompts = [
    'a photo of sks dog in a Renaissance painting style',
    'a photo of sks dog wearing astronaut suit on the moon, 4K',
    'oil painting of sks dog by Van Gogh, thick impasto brushstrokes',
]
for prompt in prompts:
    img = pipe(prompt, num_inference_steps=50, guidance_scale=7.5,
                generator=torch.Generator('cuda').manual_seed(42)).images[0]
    img.save(prompt[:40].replace(' ','_') + '.png')
    print(f'Saved: {prompt[:50]}')

title: Production Speech Transcription with Whisper
desc: Build a production speech transcription service using faster-whisper (4x faster than OpenAI Whisper). Real-time streaming, word-level timestamps, VAD filtering, and FastAPI endpoint.

sections.body: faster-whisper uses CTranslate2 for 4x faster inference than the original Whisper, with word-level timestamps and confidence scores.

code.label: Python - batch transcription with word timestamps
code.src:
from faster_whisper import WhisperModel
import time

model = WhisperModel(
    'large-v3', device='cuda', compute_type='float16', num_workers=4
)

def transcribe(audio_path: str, language: str = None) -> dict:
    t0 = time.time()
    segments, info = model.transcribe(
        audio_path,
        language=language,         # None = auto-detect
        beam_size=5,
        word_timestamps=True,
        vad_filter=True,           # skip silence
        vad_parameters={'min_silence_duration_ms': 500},
    )
    result = [{'start': round(s.start,3), 'end': round(s.end,3),
                'text': s.text.strip(),
                'words': [{'w':w.word,'start':w.start,'end':w.end,'prob':round(w.probability,3)}
                           for w in (s.words or [])]}
               for s in segments]
    elapsed = time.time()-t0
    print(f'RTF: {elapsed/info.duration:.2f}x | Lang: {info.language}')
    return {'segments': result, 'language': info.language}

result = transcribe('interview.mp3')
for seg in result['segments'][:3]:
    print(f'[{seg["start"]:.1f}s] {seg["text"]}')

sections.body: Wrap the transcription model in a FastAPI endpoint with file upload, request validation, and async processing for concurrent requests.

code.label: Python - FastAPI transcription API
code.src:
from fastapi import FastAPI, UploadFile, HTTPException
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
import tempfile, os

app = FastAPI(title='Whisper Transcription API')

ALLOWED_TYPES = {'audio/mpeg','audio/wav','audio/mp4','audio/webm','audio/ogg'}

@app.post('/transcribe')
async def transcribe_endpoint(file: UploadFile, language: str = None):
    if file.content_type not in ALLOWED_TYPES:
        raise HTTPException(400, f'Unsupported type: {file.content_type}')
    if file.size and file.size > 100 * 1024 * 1024:  # 100MB
        raise HTTPException(413, 'File too large (max 100MB)')

    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.audio', delete=False) as tmp:
        tmp.write(await file.read())
        tmp_path = tmp.name

    try:
        result = transcribe(tmp_path, language=language)
        full_text = ' '.join(s['text'] for s in result['segments'])
        return JSONResponse({
            'text':     full_text,
            'language': result['language'],
            'segments': result['segments'],
            'word_count': len(full_text.split())
        })
    finally:
        os.unlink(tmp_path)

# uvicorn app:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --workers 2
